evidence: "One of best Westerns I've seen; makes John Wayne's westerns look like comedies."
"Great action with great insights to the trials and tribulations of the military family."
"Not the greatest western ever produced; still entertaining."
"We normally watch two episodes per night - we have had to watch a couple of episodes twice mainly because we missed something in those episodes."
"Had to sacrifice sleep time so we watch the series -- great series, tough language; but don't let that stop you."
"Great series - I don't know any of the actors -- but who cares; they're great."
"The portrayal of the town is exactly how I would picture an old mining town in the late 1880's."
"I don't know any of the actors -- but who cares; they're great."
"I had preconceived thoughts about this series; i.e. John Wayne typical movie -- I was wrong!"
"Great action with great insights to the trials and tribulations of the military family."
"The portrayal of the town is exactly how I would picture an old mining town in the late 1880's."
"I highly recommend this to all, especially our young - they can actually visualize what their Great-Great Grandparents went through in the name of freedom."
"CAUTION: keep the children out of the room while watching."

summary: The user enjoys westerns and military dramas, appreciating strong narratives and visual storytelling. They show a preference for slower pacing and high production quality, while their brand loyalty to specific creators is low. Emotional resonance is significant in their viewing experience, indicating a desire for impactful stories.
